commands.repair_reason: Manual review is needed for live Telegram proof and acceptance of the 300s wait tradeoff; there is no narrow automated code repair to request.; Cleared: The diff only changes reply admission code and a focused test; it does not touch dependencies, workflows, secrets, package metadata, or other security-sensitive execution surfaces. commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a 300s bounded active-reply wait for visible dispatch admission and a Telegram forum-topic regression test.

PR surface: Source +8, Tests +46. Total +54 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main calls admitReplyTurn from dispatch without waitTimeoutMs, so replyRunRegistry.waitForIdle uses its 15s default before active-run skip. I did not execute the runtime probe in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Manual review is needed for live Telegram proof and acceptance of the 300s wait tradeoff; there is no narrow automated code repair to request.

target.title: fix(reply): queue visible turns behind active replies
target.body:
## Summary
- Keep visible reply dispatches waiting up to 300s behind an active same-session reply operation instead of falling through the 15s `reply-operation-active` skip path.
- Leave heartbeat admission behavior unchanged so background/context-engine turns still skip while a visible reply owns the lane.
- Add a Telegram forum-topic regression for message `2444` shape that verifies visible dispatch uses a bounded 300s active-run wait and reaches the reply resolver.

## Verification
- `node --import tsx - <<'EOF' ... runtime admission probe ... EOF`
- `CI=1 OPENCLAW_TEST_WORKERS=1 pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts --pool=threads --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=dot -t "Telegram topic"`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`
- live gateway restart and Telegram topic ingress proof on head `a47837f9407e109aaa1ec5c2a75470110e7a7d63`

## Real Behavior Proof
Behavior addressed: A visible Telegram forum-topic user message arriving while a same-session heartbeat/background reply operation is active should wait for the active operation to clear instead of being skipped as `reply-operation-active` after the default 15s admission timeout. The wait is intentionally bounded to 300s so leaked active operations recover as `reply-operation-active` instead of leaving the visible turn pending indefinitely.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout with the patched source loaded through Node/tsx, plus the deployed OpenClaw gateway rebuilt and restarted on PR head `a47837f9407e109aaa1ec5c2a75470110e7a7d63`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx` runtime probe created an active reply operation for `agent:main:telegram:group:-1003774691294:topic:3731`, completed it after 50ms, then admitted a visible turn with `waitTimeoutMs: 300_000`. Keshav then rebuilt and restarted the deployed gateway on PR head `a47837f9407e109aaa1ec5c2a75470110e7a7d63`, and a live Telegram forum-topic message was received by that restarted gateway.

Evidence after fix: Console output from the local OpenClaw runtime probe and redacted runtime log excerpts from the deployed gateway:

```text
runtime-probe: visible admission status=owned elapsedMs=51
runtime-probe: adoptedSessionId=active-session
runtime-probe: activeAfter=false
```

```text
$ jq -r '.gitCommit // .commit // .revision // empty' dist/build-info.json
a47837f9407e109aaa1ec5c2a75470110e7a7d63

$ systemctl --user show openclaw-gateway.service --property=MainPID,ActiveState,SubState,ExecMainStartTimestamp
MainPID=652729
ExecMainStartTimestamp=Wed 2026-05-27 07:41:11 IST
ActiveState=active
SubState=running
```

```text
May 27 07:41:11 systemd[831]: Started openclaw-gateway.service - OpenClaw Gateway (v2026.5.26).
May 27 07:41:20 node[652729]: [gateway] http server listening (...; 7.6s)
May 27 07:41:21 node[652729]: [gateway] ready
May 27 07:41:52 node[652729]: [telegram] Inbound message telegram:group:-1003966283270:topic:547 -> @Klaw18_bot (group, 5 chars)
May 27 07:41:58 node[652729]: [agent/embedded] codex app-server context-engine projection decision
May 27 07:42:02 node[652729]: [agent/embedded] codex app-server wrote context-engine thread binding
```

Observed result after fix: The visible Telegram-topic turn in the runtime probe waited for the active operation, adopted the active session id, and left no active reply operation behind after completion. The rebuilt deployed gateway is running the exact PR head and live Telegram forum-topic messages traverse the gateway/agent admission path on that build. The focused Telegram-topic dispatch tests also passed and confirmed heartbeat turns still skip while the lane is active.

What was not tested: I did not force a live deployed Telegram message to wait behind a deliberately held active operation for the full 300s timeout. The 300s active-wait argument and heartbeat skip behavior are covered by the runtime proof and focused Telegram-topic dispatch regression; the live gateway proof confirms the latest head was built, restarted, and receiving Telegram forum-topic traffic.
